articles.description: Rejected by Arsenal as a teenager, Federico Valverde has transformed himself into a Real Madrid legend - and was the main man in their Champions League win over Manchester City last week.
articles.title: Arsenal trial and self-doubt: How Federico Valverde became a Real Madrid legend

articles.description: Sports editor Dan Roan analyses whether Chelsea's record fine for a raft of Premier League rule breaches was lenient.
articles.title: Chelsea fine: Was Premier League punishment lenient?

articles.description: The president of Iran's football federation says the organisation is negotiating with Fifa to move the country's World Cup matches from the United States to Mexico.
articles.title: World Cup 2026: Iran 'negotiating' with Fifa to move games from US to Mexico

articles.description: Champions League LIVE coverage as Igor Tudor, Arne Slot and Eddie Howe speak to the media before the last-16 matches.
articles.title: Champions League LIVE: Manager news conferences, team news and previews

articles.description: Tottenham defender Micky van de Ven says it is "the biggest nonsense" to suggest the club's players do not care about the team's current plight.
articles.title: Micky van de Ven: Tottenham defender says 'biggest nonsense' to claim players do not care about club's struggles

articles.description: BBC Sport F1 correspondent Andrew Benson answers your questions following the Chinese Grand Prix.
articles.title: F1 Q&A: Lewis Hamilton, Ferrari's starts, rule changes and will McLaren and Red Bull benefit from month-long break?

articles.description: The BBC does not plan to use four-time Olympic gold medallist Michael Johnson in its athletics coverage this year.
articles.title: Michael Johnson: No plans for BBC to use American in athletics coverage

articles.description: Tottenham, Chelsea and Manchester City all need something special to stay in the Champions League, but sides have recovered from three-goal deficits before.
articles.title: Champions League comebacks: Hope for Manchester City, Chelsea and Tottenham before second-leg ties

articles.description: Captain Sam Kerr scores the winner as Australia beat China 2-1 to reach the final of the Asian Cup.
articles.title: Sam Kerr scores winner as Australia beat China in Asian Cup

articles.description: Brighton should have been awarded a penalty in their 1-0 loss at home to Arsenal on 4 March, says the Premier League's KMI Panel.
articles.title: VAR error cost Brighton a penalty against Arsenal
